answer: Pinterest utilizes the Model Context Protocol (MCP) to connect AI agents with internal data and tools through a unified standard rather than bespoke integrations [1][2]. Their architecture favors cloud-hosted, domain-specific MCP servers—such as those for Presto, Spark, and internal knowledge—managed via a central registry and a unified deployment pipeline [1][3]. This ecosystem enforces strict governance, including security reviews and two-layer authorization using JWTs and mesh identities [1][3][2]. While Pinterest focuses on an internal, production-grade ecosystem, various third-party MCP servers also exist for interacting with public Pinterest data [4][5][6].
